essence: Builds the structural backbone of buildings through precision, strength, and time‑tested craftsmanship.

identity.title: Bricklayer
identity.summary: Constructs walls, foundations, and structural elements by laying bricks, blocks, and mortar with accuracy and skill.

salary.notes: High‑skill bricklayers, site supervisors, and self‑employed tradespeople can exceed £50k–£80k+ depending on demand and project scale.

qualifications: NVQ or Diploma in Trowel Occupations
Strong Understanding of Construction Techniques and Materials
Ability to Read Plans and Work to Precise Measurements

lifestyle.description: Bricklayers work outdoors on construction sites where physical strength, endurance, and precision are essential. Days involve laying bricks or blocks, mixing mortar, checking levels and alignment, and building structural elements that must be both strong and accurate. The work is physically demanding and weather‑dependent, often requiring early starts and steady pacing throughout the day. Despite the challenges, the role offers deep satisfaction — every wall, building, or extension stands as a visible testament to the bricklayer’s skill. This lifestyle suits individuals who enjoy hands‑on work, craftsmanship, and contributing to structures that last for decades.

habits: Prepares mortar to the correct mix
